mnemonics: Timeline TIDES: Tabular (1960s), Integrated (1970s), Distributed (1980s), Enterprise (1990s), Semi-structured (2000s)
keyPoints: Hierarchical and network models were early systems requiring complex navigation paths.
The relational model, introduced by E.F. Codd, revolutionized databases with simple tables and SQL.
The evolution was driven by needs for data independence, reduced redundancy, and support for complex data types.
commonMistakes: Believing relational databases were the first type of database system.
Thinking that NoSQL databases are inherently faster than SQL databases for all use cases.
Confusing a Database Management System (DBMS) with a database itself (the data).
